tasks.title: Research and redesign cohort request cards
tasks.objective: Define and implement a more useful public cohort card layout for potential participants, including capacity cues, better image proportions, and local-time display.
authority: Use current UX research or credible design references before finalizing the card layout.
Prioritize participant decision needs over decorative imagery.
Represent capacity/quorum/open spot information accurately from repository data.
Prefer viewer-local time rendering for visible cohort times, with accessible fallback behavior.
Do not add feedback tasks to the main task ledger.
acceptance_criteria: Public cohort cards clearly communicate capacity state, including enough information to understand min quorum, max participants, and remaining/open spots or an equivalent clearer presentation.
Card images no longer dominate the card at the expense of title, topic, timing, quorum, and action-relevant details.
Visible card time is rendered in the viewer's local timezone in the browser, with a stable fallback for non-JavaScript contexts.
The card layout remains responsive and does not overlap or truncate critical decision text on mobile or desktop.
Automated tests and a browser smoke check cover the updated card content and layout-critical behavior.
verification: Run focused event browsing and MVP verification tests.
Run `npm run check` before issue closeout.
Run a browser smoke check for `/cohorts` at mobile and desktop widths.
tasks.area: public feed, cohort cards, local time, capacity UX
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when cohort cards better support participant decisions, local time is shown, and verification passes.
tasks.evidence: 2026-06-12 22:08 EDT: Added service-level capacity summaries for public events, redesigned feed cards around compact images and stable decision fields for starts/open spots/quorum, enhanced card/detail times to browser-local display with UTC fallback, and verified with focused tests, browser smoke at desktop and 390px mobile, lint, and `npm run check`.
